title: Dual Runtime Architecture
description: Support both container and Firecracker runtime modes for flowspec-agents
rationale: Container mode provides fast development iteration with devcontainer. Firecracker mode provides VM-level security isolation for production. This pattern is proven by AWS Lambda and Kata Containers.
impact: Requires maintaining both deployment paths and testing in both modes

title: Monolithic Image with Multi-Entrypoint
description: Single flowspec-agents Docker image containing all agent types, with runtime selection via entrypoint
rationale: flowspec-agents has unified codebase where agents differ only in prompts/configs (15 .md files, ~200KB). All agents share Python runtime and dependencies. Per-agent images would duplicate 99% of content for minimal benefit.
alternatives_considered: Per-agent images (rejected: artificial separation, code is shared)
Layered images with base + agent layers (rejected: unnecessary complexity)
impact: Larger single image (~300-500MB) but simpler deployment and image management

title: Tiered Rootfs Variants
description: Build 2-3 rootfs image variants for different agent capability requirements
rationale: Not all agents need container runtime. Tech-writer and researcher need only Python and basic tools (~50MB). Platform-engineer and SRE agents need containerd for container building (~150MB). Tiered approach optimizes for common case while supporting advanced use cases.
alternatives_considered: Single rootfs with all tools (rejected: wasteful for basic agents)
Per-agent rootfs images (rejected: too many images to manage)
impact: nanofuse must map agent_type to appropriate rootfs variant

title: Pre-baked Rootfs Extraction
description: Extract flowspec-agents Docker image into Firecracker rootfs at build time, not runtime
rationale: Firecracker provides VM-level isolation. Additional container isolation is unnecessary overhead. Pre-baking enables fastest boot (~150ms), smallest attack surface (no daemon), and lowest resource usage. Docker image used as packaging format only.
alternatives_considered: Docker-in-Docker (rejected: heavy, slow, large attack surface)
impact: Docker image is build-time dependency, not runtime

title: Agent Selection via Environment Variable
description: Pass agent type selection to VM via AGENT_TYPE environment variable
rationale: Simple, standard Linux mechanism. Agent type is small data (string). Environment variables are easily set via Firecracker boot params.
alternatives_considered: Metadata service (rejected: unnecessary complexity for MVP)
impact: Entrypoint script must handle AGENT_TYPE routing

title: Task Communication via Mounted Drive
description: Pass task details and collect results via Firecracker mounted drive, not environment variables
rationale: Task details can be large (code context, files). Environment variables have ~128KB limit. Mounted drives have no size limit, support binary files, and are standard Firecracker feature. More secure than environment (not in process list).
impact: nanofuse must prepare task directory before VM spawn and read results after completion

title: AI Proxy as Middleware Library (Phase 1)
description: Implement AI logging and basic security as Python middleware library within flowspec-agents, not separate service
rationale: For MVP, logging and basic metrics don't require separate enforcement boundary. Python middleware is standard pattern for observability. Avoids complexity of multi-process management or separate VM. Can evolve to separate service if enforcement needs emerge.

phase_2.trigger: Need for cost enforcement or compliance
phase_2.approach: Separate VM (sidecar pattern) or host-based proxy

alternatives_considered: Separate VM sidecar (deferred: over-engineering for MVP)
In-VM separate process (rejected: needs process manager)
impact: flowspec-agents must integrate middleware; observability handled via VM telemetry to host

title: Ephemeral VMs with External State
description: VMs are stateless and ephemeral; all persistent state written to mounted drives
rationale: Aligns with immutable infrastructure principles. VM crashes don't lose data. Enables VM reuse across tasks (warm pools). Simplifies cleanup. State on mounted drive can be backed up, versioned, audited.

implementation.recovery: nanofuse monitors mounted drive for results

alternatives_considered: Stateful VMs with persistent disk (rejected: violates immutability)
impact: Task state must be explicitly written to mounted drive; nanofuse manages state lifecycle

title: No systemd in Rootfs
description: Use lightweight init (tini) instead of systemd for process management
rationale: Single-process workload (flowspec-agent) doesn't need systemd complexity. Alpine uses OpenRC by default anyway. tini provides signal handling and zombie reaping in <1MB. systemd adds 10-20MB and boot time overhead.
alternatives_considered: OpenRC (rejected: more complex than needed for single process)
impact: Cannot use systemd-dependent services; must handle logging without journald

title: flowspec-agents Alpine Base Unchanged
description: Existing flowspec-agents Docker image (Alpine-based) requires no changes
rationale: Image is already well-structured for extraction. Alpine is lightweight and security-focused. Build process extracts existing image rather than rebuilding. Maintains compatibility with container runtime mode.
impact: Build pipeline uses docker export to extract existing image; no flowspec-agents code changes needed

title: Task Result Communication Evolution
description: File-based results for MVP, evolving to nanofuse log collector API for aggregation
rationale: File-based (`/mnt/results/`) is simplest for MVP and consistent with task input mechanism. Provides clear migration path to centralized logging without blocking initial implementation. nanofuse can monitor mounted drive and forward to collector API.

implementation.phase_2: nanofuse log collector API aggregates and forwards logs

impact: Initial simplicity, planned evolution to centralized observability

title: Secrets Management Strategy
description: Short-lived GitHub tokens and subscription-based cached logins via environment variables and mounted secrets
rationale: Minimal sensitive data in VMs. Short-lived GitHub tokens (refreshable) reduce blast radius. Subscription cached logins avoid repeated authentication. Environment variables for short-lived tokens, mounted secrets for exceptions (large credentials, binary tokens).
secrets_types: Optional LLM API keys (supported but not preferred)

delivery_methods.mounted_secrets: Large credentials, binary tokens, exception cases

alternatives_considered: All mounted secrets (rejected: environment simpler for tokens)
impact: Secrets delivery varies by type; ephemeral VMs + short-lived creds = minimal risk

title: Network Proxy via nanofuse-gateway
description: Transparent proxy through nanofuse-gateway for logging and security, deployed in-VM
rationale: nanofuse-gateway runs as separate process within same Firecracker microVM. All agent traffic proxied through gateway for logging, domain allow-listing, rate limiting, and security monitoring. Simpler than host-side networking while providing visibility and control.

deployment.runtime: Flattened into same Firecracker microVM rootfs

alternatives_considered: Full isolation (rejected: agents need package managers, git)
impact: Requires nanofuse-gateway image build and extraction; all traffic visible to host

title: Pre-installed Packages Strategy
description: Fat rootfs with common toolchains and packages pre-installed
rationale: Sub-200ms boot time incompatible with on-demand package installation. Most AI coding tasks use predictable dependencies (Python, Node.js, common libs). Fat rootfs (~200-300MB) enables fast startup with reproducible environment. Exception-based on-demand installation available for rare packages.
pre_installed: Python 3.x + pip + common packages (requests, pytest, etc.)
Node.js + npm + common packages (typescript, jest, etc.)
on_demand: Exception cases only (rare packages, user-specific deps)
alternatives_considered: On-demand all packages (rejected: slow, network dependency)
Shared package cache (rejected: complexity for MVP)
impact: Larger rootfs but predictable, fast startup; 90%+ tasks have zero install time

title: Persistent Per-User Workspace
description: Per-user persistent workspace with git-based state management
rationale: Users expect IDE-like persistent workspace. Git provides natural state checkpoint mechanism. Mounted workspace at `/workspace/<user-id>` persists across sessions. Primary persistence via git commits pushed to remote branches, with git worktree for multi-branch workflows.
impact: Storage management needed; enables multi-session workflows

title: Host-Managed VM Lifecycle
description: VM lifecycle controlled by nanofuse API commands only, no autonomous timeouts in microVM
rationale: Keep intelligence in host (nanofuse daemon), not in microVM. Dumb VMs are simpler, more reliable, easier to maintain. Centralized policy management enables per-agent and per-user timeout configurations. Host enforces timeouts, monitors health, manages lifecycle.

implementation.monitoring: Host monitors VM exit codes and heartbeats
implementation.timeouts: Configurable per agent type, enforced host-side

alternatives_considered: In-VM timeouts (rejected: complexity, harder to update policy)
impact: Simpler microVM, centralized management, easier policy updates

title: Crash Recovery with CRIU Investigation
description: Crash detection and retry for immediate recovery, CRIU research for Phase 2 checkpointing
rationale: Host detects VM crashes via exit codes and heartbeat monitoring. Automatic retry with fresh VM provides immediate recovery. CRIU (Checkpoint/Restore In Userspace) investigated for advanced VM state snapshots to enable crash recovery without losing work. Firecracker CRIU support is experimental; application-level checkpointing (git commits) is fallback.

implementation.detection: Host monitors VM exit codes and heartbeats

criu_questions: How to snapshot filesystem safely (corruption risk)?
Can we snapshot memory only, not FS?
impact: Immediate retry provides basic recovery; CRIU research for advanced use cases

title: Cold Start with Session Token Caching
description: Fresh microVM per task (cold start) with session token reuse for UX
rationale: 200ms boot time is fast enough for cold start. Security benefits of clean VMs outweigh marginal speed gains from warm pools. Session token caching (in persistent workspace) avoids repeated logins for multi-session workflows (e.g., 5 Claude sessions = 1 login). Provides UX benefit without VM reuse complexity.

implementation.reuse: Tokens reused across sessions, not VMs

impact: Simple VM lifecycle, good security posture, acceptable UX

title: Container Images as Build Artifacts
description: flowspec-agents and nanofuse-gateway extracted from Docker images and flattened into rootfs
rationale: Docker images used as packaging format during build, not runtime. Both flowspec-agents and nanofuse-gateway extracted and flattened into single rootfs. No containerd needed for these components (run as native processes). Exception: Platform/SRE agents MAY need containerd for building user containers.
containerd_exception: Platform-engineer and sre-agent may need containerd in flowspec-container rootfs variant for building user containers
alternatives_considered: All agents need containerd (rejected: wasteful for 60% of agents)
impact: Containers are build inputs, not runtime dependencies; simplifies rootfs

title: Non-Root Agent Execution
description: All agents run as non-root user with minimal privileges
rationale: Minimal credentials in VMs. Ephemeral work datasets until validated and pushed to remote. Agents cannot modify system files, install system packages, or bind privileged ports. Rootless container builds (buildkit, podman) if needed for platform/SRE agents.
alternatives_considered: Sudo for specific commands (rejected: privilege escalation risk)
impact: Strong security boundary; some operations require workarounds (rootless builds)

title: Unrestricted Subprocess Spawning
description: Agents can spawn arbitrary subprocesses within resource limits
rationale: AI agents need to run builds, tests, git operations, package installations. Unrestricted subprocess spawning enables full workflow automation. Firecracker cgroup limits prevent resource abuse. tini reaps zombie processes. Host monitors process tree growth.
monitoring: Host tracks process tree growth and resource usage
impact: Full workflow flexibility; requires resource monitoring

title: nanofuse Log Collector API
description: Centralized log collection via nanofuse log collector API with file-based fallback
rationale: Structured logging to nanofuse log collector API (HTTP/gRPC) enables centralized aggregation and forwarding to external systems (Loki, CloudWatch, etc.). File-based logs in `/mnt/results/logs/` provide fallback and audit persistence. JSON structured format for parsing and analysis.
architecture: Agent -> nanofuse log collector API -> [Central logging + File storage]
aggregation: nanofuse forwards to central logging (Loki, CloudWatch, S3, etc.)
alternatives_considered: Syslog (rejected: less structured than HTTP API)
impact: Requires log collector API implementation in nanofuse daemon

title: SLSA 1.2 Supply Chain Security
description: SLSA Level 1.2 compliance with image signing, hash verification, and SBOMs
rationale: Supply chain security critical for rootfs images. SLSA 1.2 provides build platform attestation. Image or hash signing with cosign/sigstore ensures provenance. SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) documents all components. Signature verification before VM launch prevents tampered images.

implementation.sbom: syft or trivy for component inventory
implementation.verification: nanofuse verifies signatures before VM launch

pipeline: Build -> Generate SBOM -> Sign -> Publish with attestations -> Verify on launch
impact: Secure supply chain; requires build pipeline integration

title: Case-by-Case Data Access via MCP
description: Task-specific data mounted by default, additional access via Model Context Protocol or explicit shares
rationale: Principle of least privilege. Only task-specific data mounted in `/mnt/task/` by default. Agents can request additional context via Model Context Protocol (MCP). Explicit mounted shares for specific use cases. Full audit trail of all data access.
mcp: Agents request additional context via Model Context Protocol
audit: All data access logged to nanofuse API
impact: Secure default with escape hatches; requires MCP integration

title: Minimal Output Sanitization
description: Prevention-first approach with minimal pattern-based redaction for known secrets
rationale: Don't put sensitive data in VMs in the first place. Short-lived credentials auto-expire. Ephemeral VMs destroyed after task. Network isolation prevents exfiltration. Minimal pattern-based redaction for known patterns (API key formats, etc.). Output review catches edge cases. Trust agents within ephemeral scope.
sanitization: Minimal pattern-based redaction for known formats
trust_model: Agents trusted within ephemeral, isolated scope
impact: Simpler implementation; relies on prevention and isolation

title: Automated Rootfs Build Pipeline
description: GitHub Actions triggered by flowspec-agents and nanofuse-gateway releases
rationale: Automated pipeline ensures rootfs images are rebuilt when source images update. Triggered by flowspec-agents and nanofuse-gateway release events (tags), plus manual workflow_dispatch. Extracts Docker images, builds rootfs variants, signs with cosign, generates SBOMs, publishes to registry, and updates nanofuse config.
impact: Fully automated image lifecycle; requires GitHub Actions setup

title: Local Filesystem Image Distribution
description: Local filesystem for MVP, migrate to object storage at scale
rationale: Local filesystem (`/var/lib/nanofuse/rootfs/`) simplest for MVP. Pre-downloaded images with simple file paths. No network dependency at runtime. ~150MB images load fast from local disk. Can transparently migrate to S3/GCS with local caching without API changes. Content-addressable (hash-based) names enable deduplication.
phase_2: Object storage (S3, GCS) with local caching

features.local: Fast access, simple paths, no network

alternatives_considered: OCI registry (rejected: slower than local disk for MVP)
impact: Simple MVP, clear migration path to scale

title: Tag-Based Versioning with Config Consumption
description: Semantic versioning tags with config-driven version consumption and rollback
rationale: Immutable rootfs images versioned with semantic tags (v1.2.3). nanofuse config specifies which version to use per rootfs variant. New versions built and published with tags. Testing on subset before config update. Rollback via config change to previous tag. Enables controlled rollout and instant rollback.
config: nanofuse config specifies version per rootfs variant
deployment: Update nanofuse config to use v1.2.3
impact: Controlled updates with instant rollback; requires config management
